MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-23-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ANDREW STEINFELD'S MOTION FOR FURTHER BILL OF PARTICULARS IS TRANSFERRED TO BE HEARD IN THE DISCOVERY DEPARTMENT (ROOM 301) AT 9:00 A.M. ON JANUARY 23, 2014. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT ANDREW STEINFELD'S MOTION FOR FURTHER BILL OF PARTICULARS. THIS IS AN ACTION FOR ACCOUNT STATED ON A DELINQUENT CREDIT CARD ACCOUNT. DEFENDANT IS THE ALLEGED HOLDER OF THE CREDIT CARD. PLAINTIFF ALLEGEDLY TOOK THE DELINQUENT ACCOUNT BY ASSIGNMENT FOR COLLECTION. ON SEPTEMBER 17, 2013, DEFENDANT SERVED A BILL OF PARTICULARS SEEKING THE CREDIT CARD AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF HAS OBJECTED THAT A BILL OF PARTICULARS IS NOT THE PROPER PROCEDURAL VEHICLE FOR SEEKING THE AGREEMENT, AND ARGUES THAT IN CHOOSING THAT PROCEDURAL DEVICE DEFENDANT IMPERMISSIBLY SOUGHT TO ACCELERATE THE TIME FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM 30 DAYS (AS IN THE CASE OF A REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS) TO 10 DAYS (AS IN THE CASE OF A BILL OF PARTICULARS). NOW, OVER 4 MONTHS HAVE ELAPSED SINCE THE ORIGINAL REQUEST, WHICH PLAINTIFF DOES NOT CONTEND IS SUBSTANTIVELY IMPROPER. ACCORDINGLY, REGARDLESS OF WHETHER THE BILL OF PARTICULARS WAS THE PROPER METHOD TO REQUEST THE UNDERLYING WRITTEN AGREEMENT AND NOTE OF ASSIGNMENT ON WHICH THE ACTION IS PREMISED, THERE IS NO DISPUTE THAT THOSE DOCUMENTS ARE RELEVANT AND SUBJECT TO DISCOVERY AND PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PRODUCE THEM TO DEFENDANT WITHIN 14 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. TO THE EXTENT ANY OUTSTANDING INVOICES HAVE NOT BEEN PRODUCED, PLAINTIFF SHALL ALSO PRODUCE THEM AT THE SAME TIME. DEFENDANTS EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM GEOFFREY HOWARD, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Further Bill Of Particulars
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ANDREW STEINFELD's Motion For Further Bill Of Particulars is transferred to be heard in the Discovery Department (Room 301) at 9:00 a.m. on January 23, 2014. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Defendant Andrew Steinfeld'S Motion For Further Bill Of Particulars
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances, Pro Per Defendant Andrew Steinfeld present. (415-816-4197) / No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Andrew Steinfeld's Motion For Further Bill Of Particulars. This is an action for account stated on a delinquent credit card account. Defendant is the alleged holder of the credit card. Plaintiff allegedly took the delinquent account by assignment for collection. On September 17, 2013, Defendant served a bill of particulars seeking the credit card agreement. Plaintiff has objected that a Bill of Particulars is not the proper procedural vehicle for seeking the agreement, and argues that in choosing that procedural device defendant impermissibly sought to accelerate the time for production of documents from 30 days (as in the case of a request for production of documents) to 10 days (as in the case of a bill of particulars). Now, over 4 months have elapsed since the original request, which plaintiff does not contend is substantively improper. Accordingly, regardless of whether the bill of particulars was the proper method to request the underlying written agreement and note of assignment on which the action is premised, there is no dispute that those documents are relevant and subject to discovery and plaintiff is ordered to produce them to defendant within 14 days of this order. To the extent any outstanding invoices have not been produced, Plaintiff shall also produce them at the same time. Defendants evidentiary objections are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Geoffrey Howard, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Further Bill Of Particulars
Off Calendar Dec-23-2013 Continued To Jan-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT ANDREW STEINFELD'S Motion For Further Bill Of Particulars is continued to January 23, 2014, to give both parties a chance to comply with SF Local Rule 2.6B. Both parties are to provide courtesy copies of their filings with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. The Court only received a courtesy copy of the reply papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
